// 02 — Profile

Who I Am

I'm a Mechanical Engineering student at UC Irvine (Dean's List, expected June 2027) with hands-on experience spanning competitive FSAE engineering, UAV Forge aircraft design, independent automotive service, and product design.

I work at the intersection of structural analysis, precision manufacturing, and systems integration — building things that have to work under real load, not just in simulation.

My background as a head mechanic and business owner gives me a practical lens that most engineering students don't have — I've diagnosed failures under time pressure, managed procurement, and built teams from the ground up.

// 04 — Engineering Work

Projects

Project 01
Formula 1 Race Car — Full Assembly
SolidWorks Multi-body Assembly Aerodynamics Structural Design 20+ Subsystems
Designed a complete Formula 1 vehicle assembly in SolidWorks comprising 20+ integrated subsystems — aerodynamic bodywork, structural chassis, full suspension geometry, powertrain packaging, and a fully detailed cockpit with steering wheel interface. Every component modeled from scratch with full mate constraints, demonstrating production-level CAD proficiency. Aerodynamic surfaces shaped for front/rear downforce balance; suspension geometry laid out for correct camber and toe curves; cockpit includes a Williams-style steering wheel with display and paddle shifters.

// Achievement — XYZ Formula

X
Designed a complete F1 vehicle in SolidWorks with 20+ fully constrained subsystems
Y
Validated by zero mate conflicts and correct kinematic behavior across aero, structural, and powertrain systems
Z
By modeling each component from scratch and applying systems engineering principles to coordinate all subsystem interfaces
Project 02
FSAE Motor Mounting Assembly
SolidWorks Assembly Powertrain Integration Structural Design FSAE Electric
Designed the motor mounting system for UCI Anteater Formula Racing's FSAE electric powertrain. The assembly integrates the brushless motor, inverter tabs, and chassis hard points into a fully constrained, load-bearing interface. A radially symmetric bracket distributes drivetrain torque evenly while minimizing weight. The lightweight motor plate features a 4-arm topology with internal lightening cutouts — designed as a laser-cuttable flat pattern for fast manufacturing turnaround.

// Achievement — XYZ Formula

X
Designed a multi-interface motor mounting system achieving full positional constraint across motor, inverter, and chassis
Y
Validated structurally under drivetrain torque loads and confirmed fitment within assembly tolerance
Z
Via a radially symmetric bracket and a weight-optimized 4-arm plate designed for direct laser cutting
Project 03
Powertrain Hardware — Output Shaft & Sprocket Assembly
SolidWorks Power Transmission Turned Parts Tolerance Analysis
Two companion powertrain components for the FSAE electric drivetrain. The output shaft transfers torque from the motor through the chain reduction to the rear axle — spline engagement and bearing seats designed to spec and shown in full drivetrain context. The sprocket shaft assembly features a dual-plate mounting interface ensuring precise chain alignment and axial retention. Both designed for turning and grinding with full dimensional tolerancing for manufacturing release.

// Achievement — XYZ Formula

X
Designed output shaft and sprocket assembly for FSAE electric drivetrain power transmission
Y
Measured by correct chain alignment, bearing preload spec, and first-pass dimensional inspection compliance
Z
By tolerancing shaft shoulders, spline engagement, and sprocket hub interfaces to manufacturing-ready specification
Project 04
Suspension Pickup Bracket
SolidWorks Precision Fabrication Suspension Geometry Adjustable Design
Suspension pickup bracket designed for adjustable suspension geometry on the FSAE vehicle. The C-channel cross-section provides high bending stiffness with low mass, while slotted adjustment features allow in-field ride height and alignment tuning within a 0.95-inch envelope without component replacement. Load-bearing wall thickness sized for worst-case suspension load, with slot tolerances permitting adjustment while maintaining positional repeatability. Designed as a single billet aluminum part.

// Achievement — XYZ Formula

X
Designed a suspension pickup bracket with adjustable geometry within a 0.95-inch envelope
Y
Structurally compliant under worst-case suspension loads with field-verified alignment adjustability
Z
By integrating a C-channel section, slotted adjustment features, and tight tolerances into a single billet aluminum part
Project 05
EMRAX 228 Encoder Magnet Mount
FSAE Powertrain SolidWorks Precision Fit Tolerance Stack-Up Motor Integration
Designed and fabricated a custom encoder magnet mount for the EMRAX 228 motor used on the FSAE powertrain. The mount was made to hold the encoder magnet at the required axial offset and concentric position relative to the motor shaft, where small dimensional errors could affect signal reliability during motor testing.

XYZ Project Impact

X
Designed a custom shaft insert and encoder magnet mount for the EMRAX 228 motor under strict packaging and tolerance requirements
Y
Verified fit through CAD checks, prototype testing, and physical measurement to keep the magnet centered and positioned at the required standoff
Z
Delivered a manufacturable FSAE powertrain component under time constraints, reducing encoder integration risk and helping the team keep motor assembly moving
Project 06
Backyard Patio Speaker System
Electrical Integration PC PSU Conversion Audio Amplifier Alpine Speakers Systems Wiring
Designed and built a fully functional outdoor patio speaker system from repurposed components — converting a salvaged PC power supply into a 12V DC audio power source to drive a 4-channel amplifier and four Alpine speakers mounted throughout the patio enclosure. The project required full electrical system design: modifying the ATX PSU to output stable 12V under continuous load, sizing and routing speaker wire runs, wiring the amplifier input/output channels, and securely mounting the Alpine SXE-6926S speakers into the ceiling and wall structure. All wiring was bundled, color-coded, and routed for clean installation and future serviceability.

// Achievement — XYZ Formula

X
Built a complete outdoor audio system — 4 Alpine speakers, amplifier, and PSU-based power supply — installed in a backyard patio enclosure
Y
Measured by full-system functionality: stable 12V power delivery under load, all 4 channels driven simultaneously with clean audio output
Z
By converting a salvaged ATX PC power supply to a 12V audio rail, designing speaker wire routing, and integrating the full electrical system from power source to speaker mounts
Project 07
UAV Forge VTOL Motor Mount
UCI UAV Forge Aircraft Team VTOL Propulsion SolidWorks Motor Integration

Designed a custom VTOL motor mount for UCI UAV Forge's next competition aircraft, focusing on secure motor integration, clean alignment, and a manufacturable geometry that interfaces with the aircraft structure.

XYZ Project Impact

X
Under a competition-driven development schedule, the aircraft team needed a compact motor mount for the next VTOL platform
Y
Designed the mount around motor bolt pattern, centerline alignment, structural packaging, and assembly access in SolidWorks
Z
Produced a review-ready motor mount concept that clarified VTOL propulsion integration and created a path toward rapid prototyping for the team
